blocks.content: As soon as Amy cried out, Tess's sabre pierced through Falcoa's heart.
Following this, Rian's eyes widened as blood spurted from his mouth.
There was no mistake in Tess's swordsmanship. The sabre had accurately penetrated Falcoa's heart, and soon it would cease to function.
Falcoa sensed his impending death. He wasn't particularly frightened. Perhaps, he mused, death was the closest thing to pleasure in this world.

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Marsha.
Surprisingly, the last word from Falcoa's lips as he faced death was the name of his commander.
As if experiencing his life flashing before his eyes, the corners of his mouth faintly lifted.

blocks.speaker: Falcoa
blocks.content: Take me to the battlefield.
As Tess withdrew her sabre, Falcoa's body fell backward with a thud, collapsing, and then Rian, unable to endure the physical pain any longer, fell to his knees.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Rian! Are you okay?
Rian forced a smile as he looked back at her.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hey, Amy. Did you take care of everything?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Tell me. Where and how are you hurt? Tess! How could you stab him too?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But... there's blood coming from Rian's chest?

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What!
Tess's eyes widened as she inspected Rian, and indeed, thin streams of blood were oozing out, just as Amy had said.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Rian! Rian! Are you okay? Snap out of it!
As Tess's face paled, Rian, who had momentarily lost consciousness, waved his hand dismissively and struggled to his feet.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Don't make a fuss. It just went in a bit deep.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Rian. It's my lack of skill...

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Don't say that. If it had been any shallower, Falcoa wouldn't have fallen. We both would have been in danger then. Your judgment was right.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hmm, but...
Rian truly believed that. No matter how worried one might be for a friend, deeper was better than insufficient. That's why Tess could maintain her utmost concentration.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ugh! This wound is nothing. Let's go quickly. I'm worried about Shirone.
Even amid their fight, strange noises and explosions had been heard from inside the building.
It was clear that the commander was engaged in combat. The situation inside had to be more perilous than the battle with Falcoa.
At that moment, the wall of the building burst open, and Shirone emerged.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone!
Shirone teleported just before hitting the ground, retreating ten meters.
As his photonization state dissolved, he managed a landing posture and was pushed back along the ground.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't come closer!
The fact that the usually calm Shirone was so agitated alarmed his friends, who stood frozen in their tracks.
It's not over yet. The Sound Magic is dangerous if you get closer.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Pretty impressive. To withstand me for so long.
Bending over as she exited the building, Marsha approached with a smile.
However, her smile faded as she passed Falcoa's corpse.
Falcoa was dead.
Though he was a mentally unstable man, he had indeed lived an amusing and unrestrained life among life's misfits.
The name Parrot, which had made a mark on the continent, was imbued with his blood.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: No regrets then, Falcoa.
Marsha knew without looking. A man who might have thought dying by the sword was a far more glorious end than a heart attack induced by addiction.
But that was where her sentiment ended.
It was surprising that a mere student had taken him down, but considering Falcoa's body had been ravaged by years of drug abuse, it wasn't unusual.
Marsha refocused on confronting Shirone.
She had suspected as much when she breached the first barrier, but facing him directly confirmed that his magical combinations were exceptionally well-balanced.
His Teleportation was of a high standard, and she was pleased with the potency of his core offensive tool, the Photon Cannon.
The laser was ideal as a siege weapon, and although the defensive magic Wildfire required precise timing, it could be converted into an offensive spell if necessary.
The balance set by a mage after long contemplation and evolution.
Above all, what pleased her the most was the uniqueness of Shirone's magic, which couldn't be learned from any other mage— it was exclusively his.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Unlocker's magic, huh? Interesting. I'm starting to want it.
As Marsha mused, Shirone was also analyzing the variables of the situation, having emerged onto the open ground.
Despite numerous penalties inside the building, there were no disadvantages now.
Most importantly, there was no risk of hostages being harmed, so he could unleash his full power.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Things will be different now. I will never forgive you.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Hehe, Shirone, you will forgive me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: On what basis? Your actions have crossed the bounds of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: That's the thing, actually...

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: It was all a joke!
Shirone and his group were baffled by her words.
Then Marsha shouted towards the building.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Yuna! It's okay to come out now!
Her voice boomed, amplified, echoing towards the sea.
Moments later, the door of the building opened, and a pretty young girl walked out.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Could it be, that girl is Yuna?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Yes, that's Yuna. As you can see, she's been here comfortably without any problems.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: So, Shirone, are you not angry anymore?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh, you still don't get it? Was I just playing a prank on my little sister, and you got all worked up over that? Everything I told was a lie. So forgive me, okay?

blocks.speaker: Yuna
blocks.content: My sister did nothing wrong. In fact, Marsha saved me.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What the heck is going on? Can someone please explain this to me instead of staying silent?
It was a clear mockery. The issue was the motive. What exactly was Marsha's intention behind all this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If that's really the case... send Yuna over here.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Yuna, go back inside. I'll send you to your brother later.

blocks.speaker: Yuna
blocks.content: Okay.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you trying to do? What do you want?

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Hehe, why? Yuna is safe, isn't she? Isn't that enough? Oh, did you want to be the hero who rescues her?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's not it, but...

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Now you understand, right? I did nothing wrong. The bad one is you, Shirone. You made me sick to my stomach. That's why I'll never hand over Yuna to you.
Shirone entered his Spirit Zone.
Marsha's blade-like Attack Form was already targeting his brow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmph! I won't be taken down this time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Calm down. She's hostile toward me. That's reason enough to fight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it was a tactic of deception, he couldn't just stand by.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Plus, with the hostage safe, he wouldn't be at a disadvantage as he was inside the building.
However, breaking Shirone's expectations, Marsha deactivated her Spirit Zone and, spreading her arms as if disarming herself, said to him,

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: You can kill me if you want.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's heart sank. He understood the significance of the words she had just uttered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was the spell she had memorized each time she was beaten by her foster father.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Marsha was serious. She genuinely wanted him to end her life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you, Marsha? Who exactly are you?
Magic Association Interrogation Room.
Sakiri, a certified Grade 5 mage and investigator, wiped the sweat from his brow.
His sleeves were rolled up to his elbows, and more than half of his shirt buttons were undone.
Clearly, he was the picture of exhaustion.
However, the muscles of his arms, clutching a metal club, still bulged with apparent strength.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Whew, had a rest, shall we start again?

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Ughhh...
Lucas, who had fallen to the floor, groaned.
He was the vice-captain of the Parrot Mercenary Band, which had raided the Magic Association following Arcane.
However, at that time, he had been incapacitated by Siena's Absolute Zero magic, resulting in both legs being severed.
He had been beaten for hours.
Had he not protected his body with a Schema, he would have been dead long ago from the beating.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Here it goes. Clench your teeth.
Sakiri swung the club again.
The barrage of blows made Lucas crawl on the floor with his arms until, exhausted, he rolled over.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Aaaaah! Please stop! Ask something before hitting me! I've told you I'll tell you everything!

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Wait. We've got a thousand to reach.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: You crazy son of a bitch!
Sakiri counted internally.
Five hits left. Now four. Three.
Finally, with the last blow aimed at the ribs, he tossed the club aside with a sense of relief.
The club clattered noisily as it rolled across the floor.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Why... why are you doing this...
Lucas clutched his aching stomach and wept.
Though recruited into the downgraded Parrot Mercenary Band and quickly rising to vice-captain due to his outstanding abilities, he had been a formidable villain even in the face of Arcane.
But in front of Sakiri, he was no better than gum stuck to the floor.
Sakiri grabbed Lucas's hair and hurled him towards a chair.
Lucas crashed heavily and clung to the chair, while Sakiri sat down across the desk and said,

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Sit down, before I cut off the other arm too.
Lucas, using his arms, barely managed to seat himself.
Then, Sakiri silently scrutinized the files the investigators had compiled for a long while.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Investigator, just send me back to prison. I'll tell you where Marsha is.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: No need. What kind of woman do you think she is, to still be there?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Dammit! Then why the hell did you beat me?
As Sakiri flipped to the last page of the file, he slammed his fist on the desk in irritation.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Do you know what kind of criminal I hate the most? Murderers? Home wreckers? No, escapees.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Does the state look that easy to you? Even if you're swollen with pride, a B-grade criminal like you escaping?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Your life is over. You'll never see the sun again.

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: Aaaaah!
Lucas gulped.
Sakiri's words were not a threat but a statement of fact.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Anyway, let's start. You'd better listen well. My daughter is four years old, the age when she's supposed to be showing off in front of daddy.

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: But I haven't been home in days! Let's try to keep living. Got it?

blocks.speaker: Lucas
blocks.content: What do you want to hear?

blocks.speaker: Sakiri
blocks.content: Forget everything else, tell me about Marsha. Spill everything you know about her.
That was all Sakiri wanted to know.
Clay Marsha. Though a political criminal classified as an A-grade criminal, she hadn't caused much uproar, making her hard to capture.
The biggest problem was the scattered intelligence on her.
Sometimes she was reported as a fire mage, at other times as using ice magic.
Considering that, she was highly strategic and meticulous.
Even using all available investigators to track her had not clarified her routes of movement.
Thus, Sakiri had summoned Lucas.
From the start, the operation had been misguided.
What really needed to be known was not where Marsha was but Marsha herself.
